betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Semiconductor Engineering: P-Type Wafer Doping§f

§aP-type silicon is produced by Boron-11 ion bombardment inside a Target Chamber!§f
P-Type Doped Silicon (`qmd:semiconductor:0`) is the hole-rich semiconductor used in advanced QMD processing chains — most importantly, it is a mandatory ingredient in the §bAdvanced Processor§f (recipe `processorBasic + 4 Redstone + Hafnium Oxide + siliconPDoped → processorAdvanced`).

§6How P-Type Is Made§f:
§b• Boron-11 Ion Source§f: Feed §bDiborane§f fluid into an Accelerator Ion Source to generate a §bboron_ion§f particle beam (5 protons + 6 neutrons + 4 electrons = Boron-11). Recipe: `AcceleratorSourceRecipes.java:31` — `diborane fluid → boron_ion particles`.
§b• Beam Conditioning§f: Route the Boron-11 beam through a §bLinear Accelerator (LINAC)§f with §bRF Cavities§f and §bFocusing Electromagnets§f to reach the required energy and focus.
§b• Target Chamber Bombardment§f: Per `TargetChamberRecipes.java:830`, the exact recipe is:
  - Input 1: §bSilicon Wafer§f (`qmd:semiconductor:3`, 1 unit)
  - Input 2: §bBoron-11 ion particle§f (1 particle, mean energy §e≥ 600 keV§f, focus §e≥ 2.0§f)
  - Output: §bP-Type Doped Silicon§f (`qmd:semiconductor:0`)
  - RF cost: 1,000 RF per wafer; efficiency 1.0, heat 0.

§6Why You Need It§f:
P-Type is required to craft the §bAdvanced Processor§f (`qmd:semiconductor:5`), which in turn unlocks ring accelerators (§bSynchrotron Controller§f, §bBeam Diverter Controller§f, §bBeam Splitter Controller§f).

§cWarning§f: P-Type silicon is produced ONLY by this Target Chamber recipe in QMD. There is no assembler or CraftTweaker alternative — the Boron-11 beam must satisfy both the §e600 keV minimum energy§f and §efocus 2.0§f threshold or the Target Chamber will reject the input.

§eTip§f: Use the §bBeam Meter§f tool on your LINAC's output beam port to verify the Boron-11 beam has reached the §e600 keV§f and §efocus 2.0§f thresholds before it enters the Target Chamber.
